task_completion.description: Create a 3-page document with a professional theme in EdrawMax
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the EdrawMax interface with at least three page tabs (e.g., Page-1, Page-2, Page-3) visible at the bottom or side panel, and a cohesive professional color/style theme applied to the diagrams.

task_completion.description: Create a UML Class Diagram with the 6 required core domain entities
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a UML Class Diagram containing class boxes specifically named: Patient, Doctor, Appointment, Prescription, MedicalRecord, and Department.

task_completion.description: Include named attributes and multiplicity labels in the UML Class Diagram
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look inside the class boxes for listed attributes (e.g., name, ID, date) and look at the association lines between classes for multiplicity labels (e.g., 1, 0..*, 1..*).

task_completion.description: Create a UML Use Case Diagram with the 4 required actors
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a UML Use Case Diagram containing stick-figure actors specifically named: Patient, Doctor, Nurse, and Administrator.

task_completion.description: Include at least 8 distinct use cases with associations
task_completion.visual_evidence: Count the use case ovals in the Use Case Diagram; there must be at least 8 distinct ovals, and they must be connected to the actors with association lines.

task_completion.description: Create a UML Sequence Diagram for 'Schedule Appointment' with the 4 required lifelines
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a UML Sequence Diagram containing vertical lifelines specifically named: Patient, Web Interface, Appointment Service, and Database, with horizontal message-passing arrows between them.
Look at the application title bar or a 'Save As' dialog box to confirm the file is named 'ehr_uml_design.eddx' and is being saved to the /home/ga/ directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Wondershare EdrawMax GUI to create the diagrams
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show the actual EdrawMax application window, toolbars, and canvas, rather than a text editor, terminal window, or a different diagramming tool.

integrity.description: Verify the diagrams are built using actual shapes and connectors
integrity.visual_evidence: The diagrams should appear to be constructed with EdrawMax's native UML shapes, lifelines, and connectors, rather than being a single pasted image or raw text.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to generate the file
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no evidence of the agent writing a Python script or using CLI tools to bypass the GUI and generate the .eddx XML structure directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The output file must be saved exactly as /home/ga/ehr_uml_design.eddx. The document must have exactly 3 pages and use a professional theme. The UML Class Diagram must include the entities: Patient, Doctor, Appointment, Prescription, MedicalRecord, Department. The UML Use Case Diagram must include the actors: Patient, Doctor, Nurse, Administrator. The UML Sequence Diagram must include the lifelines: Patient, Web Interface, Appointment Service, Database.
